frameworks.name: Reward Prediction Error hypothesis
frameworks.one_line: Phasic midbrain dopamine signals prediction errors driving reinforcement learning.

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Learning state values from prediction errors over experienced outcomes.

frameworks.name: state representation learning
frameworks.one_line: Dopamine prediction errors also shape acquisition of adaptive task-state representations.

core_question: Can the Reward Prediction Error hypothesis be extended so dopamine signals also support learning of adaptive state representations beyond reward?
key_assumptions: Phasic dopamine activity carries reward prediction error signals
A single signal can serve associative learning and representation learning
Dopaminergic innervation of hippocampus and prefrontal cortex is functionally relevant
manipulations: Simulated tasks spanning temporal, spatial, motor, and abstract categorization domains
Dopamine signal used to update state representations rather than only values
key_findings: Extended model accounts for dopamine role in temporal and spatial representation
Extended model accounts for dopamine influence on motor response generation
Extended model accounts for dopamine role in abstract categorization
interpretations: Representation is the common theme linking dopamine functions beyond reward
Extending RPE to representation learning resolves a critical challenge to the hypothesis
